revision.comment: 新しいページ：大塚古墳（福島県須賀川市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 大塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 前田川大塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[福島県]][[須賀川市]]前田川（字大塚） |緯度度 = 37|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 0.63 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 24|経度秒 = 2.47 |ISO = JP-14 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径30m
高さ5m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 鉄器片・[[土師器]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 須賀川市指定史跡「大塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 大塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''大塚古墳'''（おおつかこふん、前田川大塚古墳）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]前田川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。須賀川市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 福島県南部、[[阿武隈川]]河岸段丘の縁辺部に築造された大型円墳である。現在までに墳丘裾部は削平を受けている。 墳形は円形で、直径約30メートル・高さ5メートルを測る{{Sfn|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘に段築は認められず、周溝の存在も明らかでない{{Sfn|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口する。玄室・玄門・羨道から構成され、長さ約8メートル・高さ約2メートルを測り、須賀川市内では[[蝦夷穴古墳]]（須賀川市和田）に継ぐ規模になる史跡説明板。。石室の石材は[[凝灰岩]]で、奥壁・床面・天井・側壁の一部には大きな板石を使用し、側壁の大部分は小さな割石を持ち送る{{Sfn|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。副葬品は明らかでなく、鉄器片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に須賀川市指定史跡に指定された。現在では石室内部への立ち入りは制限されている。 == 文化財 == === 須賀川市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 大塚古墳 - 1979年（昭和54年）7月20日指定[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html 指定・登録・認定文化財一覧]（須賀川市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（須賀川市教育委員会、1989年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=福島雅儀|year=2002|chapter=大塚古墳 > 前田川大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[蝦夷穴古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html 市指定文化財等 > 大塚古墳] - 須賀川市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつかこふん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:須賀川市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.text:
'''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) refers to various [[Kofun]] in Japan.
* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yada Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as **Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun**) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in **Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan**. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City**. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a **large circular mound** built along the edge of the **Abukuma River** terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a **diameter of approximately 30 meters** and a **height of 5 meters**{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a **yokoana-style stone chamber** (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the **southwest**. The chamber consists of a **main chamber**, **entrance**, and **passageway**, measuring about **8 meters long** and **2 meters high**, making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in **Wada, Sukagawa**)Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with **tuff rock** (*gyōkai-gan*), using **large stone slabs** for the **back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls**, while most of the **side walls** are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but **iron fragments** and **Haji pottery** have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the **late 6th century** during the **late Kofun period**. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City** in **1979** (Shōwa 54). Currently, **entry into the stone chamber is restricted** for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * **Historic Site** * **Ōtsuka Kofun** – Designated on **July 20, 1979**[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Round kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as **Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun**) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in **Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan**. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City**. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a **large circular mound** built along the edge of the **Abukuma River** terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a **diameter of approximately 30 meters** and a **height of 5 meters**{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a **yokoana-style stone chamber** (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the **southwest**. The chamber consists of a **main chamber**, **entrance**, and **passageway**, measuring about **8 meters long** and **2 meters high**, making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in **Wada, Sukagawa**)Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with **tuff rock** (*gyōkai-gan*), using **large stone slabs** for the **back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls**, while most of the **side walls** are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but **iron fragments** and **Haji pottery** have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the **late 6th century** during the **late Kofun period**. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City** in **1979** (Shōwa 54). Currently, **entry into the stone chamber is restricted** for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * **Historic Site** * **Ōtsuka Kofun** – Designated on **July 20, 1979**[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:dd]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]][[Category:dd]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as **Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun**) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in **Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan**. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City**. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a **large circular mound** built along the edge of the **Abukuma River** terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a **diameter of approximately 30 meters** and a **height of 5 meters**{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a **yokoana-style stone chamber** (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the **southwest**. The chamber consists of a **main chamber**, **entrance**, and **passageway**, measuring about **8 meters long** and **2 meters high**, making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in **Wada, Sukagawa**)Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with **tuff rock** (*gyōkai-gan*), using **large stone slabs** for the **back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls**, while most of the **side walls** are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but **iron fragments** and **Haji pottery** have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the **late 6th century** during the **late Kofun period**. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City** in **1979** (Shōwa 54). Currently, **entry into the stone chamber is restricted** for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * **Historic Site** * **Ōtsuka Kofun** – Designated on **July 20, 1979**[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as **Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun**) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in **Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan**. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City**. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a **large circular mound** built along the edge of the **Abukuma River** terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a **diameter of approximately 30 meters** and a **height of 5 meters**{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a **yokoana-style stone chamber** (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the **southwest**. The chamber consists of a **main chamber**, **entrance**, and **passageway**, measuring about **8 meters long** and **2 meters high**, making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in **Wada, Sukagawa**)Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with **tuff rock** (*gyōkai-gan*), using **large stone slabs** for the **back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls**, while most of the **side walls** are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but **iron fragments** and **Haji pottery** have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the **late 6th century** during the **late Kofun period**. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City** in **1979** (Shōwa 54). Currently, **entry into the stone chamber is restricted** for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * **Historic Site** * **Ōtsuka Kofun** – Designated on **July 20, 1979**[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == Related Sites == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as **Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun**) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in **Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan**. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City**. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a **large circular mound** built along the edge of the **Abukuma River** terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a **diameter of approximately 30 meters** and a **height of 5 meters**{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a **yokoana-style stone chamber** (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the **southwest**. The chamber consists of a **main chamber**, **entrance**, and **passageway**, measuring about **8 meters long** and **2 meters high**, making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in **Wada, Sukagawa**)Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with **tuff rock** (*gyōkai-gan*), using **large stone slabs** for the **back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls**, while most of the **side walls** are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but **iron fragments** and **Haji pottery** have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the **late 6th century** during the **late Kofun period**. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City** in **1979** (Shōwa 54). Currently, **entry into the stone chamber is restricted** for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * **Historic Site** * **Ōtsuka Kofun** – Designated on **July 20, 1979**[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as **Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun**) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in **Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan**. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City**. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a **large circular mound** built along the edge of the **Abukuma River** terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a **diameter of approximately 30 meters** and a **height of 5 meters**{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a **yokoana-style stone chamber** (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the **southwest**. The chamber consists of a **main chamber**, **entrance**, and **passageway**, measuring about **8 meters long** and **2 meters high**, making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in **Wada, Sukagawa**)Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with **tuff rock** (*gyōkai-gan*), using **large stone slabs** for the **back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls**, while most of the **side walls** are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but **iron fragments** and **Haji pottery** have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the **late 6th century** during the **late Kofun period**. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City** in **1979** (Shōwa 54). Currently, **entry into the stone chamber is restricted** for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * **Historic Site** * **Ōtsuka Kofun** – Designated on **July 20, 1979**[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as **Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun**) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in **Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan**. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City**. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a **large circular mound** built along the edge of the **Abukuma River** terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a **diameter of approximately 30 meters** and a **height of 5 meters**{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a **yokoana-style stone chamber** (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the **southwest**. The chamber consists of a **main chamber**, **entrance**, and **passageway**, measuring about **8 meters long** and **2 meters high**, making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in **Wada, Sukagawa**)Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with **tuff rock** (*gyōkai-gan*), using **large stone slabs** for the **back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls**, while most of the **side walls** are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but **iron fragments** and **Haji pottery** have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the **late 6th century** during the **late Kofun period**. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a **historic site** by **Sukagawa City** in **1979** (Shōwa 54). Currently, **entry into the stone chamber is restricted** for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * **Historic Site** * **Ōtsuka Kofun** – Designated on **July 20, 1979**[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]]

Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{disambiguation]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otsuka Kofun (disambiguation)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]]

{{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a circular tumulus (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. ## Overview Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. ## Cultural Significance The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. ## Cultural Heritage ### Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. ## References {{Reflist}} ## Bibliography * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} ## Related Sites * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} ## External Links {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture.
