Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | shape = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | size = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | artifacts = Iron fragments, [[Haji ware|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | designation = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | icon = Enpun | label = Ōtsuka Kofun | label_position= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historical Site Information Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was designated as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Cultural Heritage == === Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Cultural Properties] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical Site Information Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那須郡那珂川町]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:那須小川古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture.
