Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=draft}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}, Kyoto Prefecture. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=draft|12=draft}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}} {{draft categories| |12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Ōtsuka Kofun}} {{citation needed|Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[ja:那須郡那珂川町]] [[ja:那須小川古墳群]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:宇都宮市]] [[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] [[ja:川島町]] [[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] [[ja:さいたま市]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[ja:世田谷区]] [[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] [[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]] [[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] [[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]] [[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] [[ja:大垣市]] [[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] [[ja:本巣市]] [[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] [[ja:名古屋市守山区]] [[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊田市]] [[ja:須恵器]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:向日市]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[en:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]] [[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 大塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 前田川大塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[福島県]][[須賀川市]]前田川（字大塚） |緯度度 = 37|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 0.63 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 24|経度秒 = 2.47 |ISO = JP-14 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径30m
高さ5m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 鉄器片・[[土師器]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 須賀川市指定史跡「大塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 大塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''大塚古墳'''（おおつかこふん、前田川大塚古墳）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]前田川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。須賀川市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 福島県南部、[[阿武隈川]]河岸段丘の縁辺部に築造された大型円墳である。現在までに墳丘裾部は削平を受けている。 墳形は円形で、直径約30メートル・高さ5メートルを測る{{Sfn|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘に段築は認められず、周溝の存在も明らかでない{{Sfn|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口する。玄室・玄門・羨道から構成され、長さ約8メートル・高さ約2メートルを測り、須賀川市内では[[蝦夷穴古墳]]（須賀川市和田）に継ぐ規模になる史跡説明板。。石室の石材は[[凝灰岩]]で、奥壁・床面・天井・側壁の一部には大きな板石を使用し、側壁の大部分は小さな割石を持ち送る{{Sfn|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。副葬品は明らかでなく、鉄器片・[[土師器]]片のみが検出されている{{Sfn|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に須賀川市指定史跡に指定された。現在では石室内部への立ち入りは制限されている。 == 文化財 == === 須賀川市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 大塚古墳 - 1979年（昭和54年）7月20日指定[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html 指定・登録・認定文化財一覧]（須賀川市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（須賀川市教育委員会、1989年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=福島雅儀|year=2002|chapter=大塚古墳 > 前田川大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|前田川大塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[蝦夷穴古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html 市指定文化財等 > 大塚古墳] - 須賀川市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつかこふん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:須賀川市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m

It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en}}. revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} =External links== {{Commonscat|Otsuka {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter.
