{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture.
