{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Sukagawa|qid=Q819664}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.250166666666665|N|140.40069444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q113490541]] Wikidata name: Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. [[sn:commons:Category:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Sukagawa|WD=Q819664}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.250166666666665|N|140.40069444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q113490541]] Wikidata name: Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|qq=|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Sukagawa|WD=Q819664}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.250166666666665|N|140.40069444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q113490541]] Wikidata name: Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} Historic Site1 as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} =External links== {{Commonscat|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002518.html Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site: Ōtsuka Kofun] (Sukagawa City website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚古墳 (須賀川市)|version=90919494|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[ja:那須郡那珂川町]] [[ja:那須小川古墳群]] [[ja:戸祭大塚古墳]] [[ja:宇都宮市]] [[ja:下栗大塚古墳]] [[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] [[ja:川島町]] [[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] [[ja:さいたま市]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[ja:世田谷区]] [[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] [[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]] [[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] [[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]] [[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] [[ja:大垣市]] [[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] [[ja:本巣市]] [[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] [[ja:名古屋市守山区]] [[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊田市]] [[ja:須恵器]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:向日市]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sukagawa]] {{Commons category|Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)}} {{wikidata link|Q113490541}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)]][[ja:大塚古墳 (須賀川市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Sukagawa|WD=Q819664}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.250166666666665|N|140.40069444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q113490541]] Wikidata name: Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun | name = Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Otsuka Kofun (Sukagawa), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima]], [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa City]], Maedagawa (Ōtsuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|37|15|0.63|N|140|24|2.47|E|region:JP-14}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound) | area = Diameter 30m
Height 5m | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] | burial goods = Iron fragments, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | companion = | construction = Late [[6th century]] | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site ("Ōtsuka Kofun") | map = Japan Fukushima | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = Ōtsuka Kofun | map_dot_label= top }} '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳, also known as '''Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun''') is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Maedagawa, Sukagawa City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a [[Round Kofun|circular tumulus]] (*enpun*) and has been Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City'''. == Overview == Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''large circular mound''' built along the edge of the '''Abukuma River''' terrace in southern Fukushima Prefecture. Over time, the base of the mound has suffered erosion. The tumulus has a '''diameter of approximately 30 meters''' and a '''height of 5 meters'''{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No evidence of tiered construction or a surrounding moat has been confirmed{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial chamber is a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens towards the '''southwest'''. The chamber consists of a '''main chamber''', '''entrance''', and '''passageway''', measuring about '''8 meters long''' and '''2 meters high''', making it the second-largest stone chamber in Sukagawa after {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} (located in '''Wada, Sukagawa''')Historic Site Intypeation Board.. The stone chamber is constructed with '''tuff rock''' (*gyōkai-gan*), using '''large stone slabs''' for the '''back wall, floor, ceiling, and part of the side walls''', while most of the '''side walls''' are made of small stacked stones{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No significant burial items have been identified, but '''iron fragments''' and '''Haji pottery''' have been found{{Sfn|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Based on the construction style, the kofun is believed to date to the '''late 6th century''' during the '''late Kofun period'''. == Cultural Significance == The tumulus was Historic Site1 as a '''historic site''' by '''Sukagawa City''' in '''1979''' (Shōwa 54). Currently, '''entry into the stone chamber is restricted''' for preservation purposes. == Historic Site == === Sukagawa City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' – Designated on '''July 20, 1979'''[https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1007558.html List of Designated, Registered, and Recognized Historic Site] (Sukagawa City website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Sukagawa City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Masayoshi Fukushima|year=2002|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Maedagawa Ōtsuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emishi-ana Kofun|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi-ana Kofun|1=Ezoana Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110906544}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture.
